title: Brachyloma elongatum

This family is part of the larger taxonomic group known as Ericales. intro: Brachyloma elongatum, commonly known as the 'Mountain Heath', is a small, prostrate shrub native to eastern Australia, characterized by its tiny, pink or white flowers and small, ovate-shaped leaves. The Mountain Heath has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
